fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

the genome fetch and most commonly used indexer DMs to normalize the on-disk layout as proposed in https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/discussions/19013.

In addition:

- For those that had Python wrappers, I dropped the wrappers. In some cases this avoids building a special mulled container just for the DM
- Updated some underlying tool versions
- Added some tests of non-default options
- For the STAR DM, automatically calculate the `--genomeSAindexNbases` and `--genomeChrBinNbits` options as recommended by the manual, to drastically reduce the index size for small genomes.

the genome fetch and most commonly used indexer DMs to normalize the on-disk layout as proposed in https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/discussions/19013.

In addition:

- For those that had Python wrappers, I dropped the wrappers. In some cases this avoids building a special mulled container just for the DM
- Updated some underlying tool versions
- Added some tests of non-default options
- For the STAR DM, automatically calculate the `--genomeSAindexNbases` and `--genomeChrBinNbits` options as recommended by the manual, to drastically reduce the index size for small genomes.

the genome fetch and most commonly used indexer DMs to normalize the on-disk layout as proposed in https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/discussions/19013.

In addition:

- For those that had Python wrappers, I dropped the wrappers. In some cases this avoids building a special mulled container just for the DM
- Updated some underlying tool versions
- Added some tests of non-default options
- For the STAR DM, automatically calculate the `--genomeSAindexNbases` and `--genomeChrBinNbits` options as recommended by the manual, to drastically reduce the index size for small genomes.

FOR CONTRIBUTOR:

the genome fetch and most commonly used indexer DMs to normalize the on-disk layout as proposed in https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/discussions/19013.

In addition:

- For those that had Python wrappers, I dropped the wrappers. In some cases this avoids building a special mulled container just for the DM
- Updated some underlying tool versions
- Added some tests of non-default options
- For the STAR DM, automatically calculate the `--genomeSAindexNbases` and `--genomeChrBinNbits` options as recommended by the manual, to drastically reduce the index size for small genomes.

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19255","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19255/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19255/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19255/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19255","id":2719526847,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc6iGK-_","number":19255,"title":"[help_wanted] File downloads failing from test S3 backends in the object store","user":{"login":"cat-bro","id":22670687,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIyNjcwNjg3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/22670687?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro","html_url":"https://github.com/cat-bro","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-12-05T06:59:17Z","updated_at":"2024-12-05T19:28:05Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Galaxy Australia is considering adding S3 storage for some user data. The purpose would be for the storage of data that is not newly created, maybe 3+ months since last access. We have been testing several options (object store, MinIO and an AWS bucket). We are running tests of how Galaxy handles access to files that are not in the cache. We are testing running jobs on datasets that are not in the cache, and downloading datasets that are not in the cache.

Results from running jobs are excellent on the local cluster and on pulsar - the jobs end up in ‘ok’ state.

Results from downloading files are concerning. I have been testing this with file sizes of 2GB, 4GB, 6GB, 8GB and 10GB. Two of the backends we are testing (ids `minio_test` and `aws_bucket_test` in object store conf) have type `boto3` and only the downloads of 2GB and 4GB files are successfully downloaded. For anything larger, attempting to download leads to timeout (504). Dataset peek is similarly affected.

For the backend connected to openstack object storage (id `pawsey_s3_test` in our object store conf) we have used backend type `generic_s3`. The downloads appear to succeed, but for sizes 4GB and above, the downloaded files are truncated to about 3GB each. It would be preferable if it failed with a timeout as it does in the other cases.

At this point our test candidates seem unusable, especially the one in which downloaded files are truncated.

I'm not sure what the solution for this should be. If I click on the floppy disk 10 minutes after the first attempt has timed out, the file has arrived in the cache and can be downloaded, so I wonder if the user can be informed that the file is not ready and advised to try again. I also wonder if the download could be directly from the storage rather than via the cache, though this would be tricky with different data types.

I’m keen to talk to others who are using or testing S3 in the Galaxy object store.

This is a link to our [object store conf](https://github.com/usegalaxy-au/infrastructure/blob/master/templates/galaxy/config/galaxy_object_store_conf.yml.j2#L174C1-L259C47) and I've pasted the relevant entries below.

```
- id: minio_test
  weight: 0
  type: boto3
  store_by: uuid
  auth:
    access_key: {{ vault_galaxy_minio_access_key }}
    secret_key: {{ vault_galaxy_minio_secret_key }}
  bucket:
    name: galaxytest
  connection:
    endpoint_url: https://platforms-s3-poc.aarnet.edu.au
  cache:
    path: /mnt/user-data-volD/minio_test_cache
    size: 50
    cache_updated_data: true
  # transfer:   # see transfer options for boto3 above in AWS configuration.
- id: aws_bucket_test
  type: boto3
  weight: 0
  store_by: uuid
  auth:
    access_key: {{ vault_aws_galaxytest_access_key }}
    secret_key: {{ vault_aws_galaxytest_secret_key }}
  bucket:
    name: ga-s3-test-230823
  connection:  # not strictly needed but more of the API works with this.
    region: ap-southeast-2
  cache:
    path: /mnt/user-data-volD/aws_bucket_test_cache
    size: 50
    cache_updated_data: true
- id: pawsey_s3_test
  type: generic_s3
  weight: 0
  store_by: uuid
  auth:
    access_key: {{ vault_pawsey_s3_test_access_key }}
    secret_key: {{ vault_pawsey_s3_test_secret_key }}
  bucket:
    name: galaxy-test
    use_reduced_redundancy: false
    max_chunk_size: 250
  connection:
    host: "projects.pawsey.org.au"
    port: 443
    is_secure: true
    conn_path: "/"
  cache:
    path: /mnt/user-data-volD/pawsey_s3_test_cache
    size: 50
    cache_updated_data: true
```

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19280","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19280/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19280/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19280/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19280","id":2723806282,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc6iWfxK","number":19280,"title":"Proposal: Read/calculate and store uncompressed size of compressed datasets","user":{"login":"natefoo","id":286969,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI4Njk2OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/286969?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo","html_url":"https://github.com/natefoo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-12-06T19:51:56Z","updated_at":"2024-12-06T19:51:56Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"A lot of TPV rules are based off input size, but input size is often a lie due to compression. This is likely a significant cause of over or underallocation of memory for jobs. I opened [an issue](https://github.com/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/141) over on the TPV repo but suspect this should probably be done internally in Galaxy especially if we don't want TPV to be expecting data to exist on disk.\r\n\r\nAs noted in the issue, the uncompressed size of gzipped data can be stored in the last 4 bytes of the file, but it is sometimes empty, in which case the only way to get it is to decompress.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19280/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19280/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

pins versions of all the `galaxy*` packages, but to mimic the current production best practice/`run.sh`, should we add all of `pinned-requirements.txt`?~~ yes

I tried adding all of `pinned-requirements.txt` to `install_requires` but it fails because `galaxy-data` 24.1.2 (and all earlier versions of it) have a pin on `social-auth-core==4.0.3` while the `pinned-requirements.txt` version for 24.1.2 is 4.5.4. We should unpin this in `galaxy-data` but it won't be possible to build a fully pinned `galaxy` metapackage until we unpin that and publish 24.1.3 packages.

## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  ```sh-session
  cd package/galaxy
  python -m build
  pip install --extra-index-url https://wheels.galaxyproject.org/simple ./dist/galaxy-24.1.2-py3-none-any.whl
  ```

pins versions of all the `galaxy*` packages, but to mimic the current production best practice/`run.sh`, should we add all of `pinned-requirements.txt`?~~ yes

I tried adding all of `pinned-requirements.txt` to `install_requires` but it fails because `galaxy-data` 24.1.2 (and all earlier versions of it) have a pin on `social-auth-core==4.0.3` while the `pinned-requirements.txt` version for 24.1.2 is 4.5.4. We should unpin this in `galaxy-data` but it won't be possible to build a fully pinned `galaxy` metapackage until we unpin that and publish 24.1.3 packages.

## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  ```sh-session
  cd package/galaxy
  python -m build
  pip install --extra-index-url https://wheels.galaxyproject.org/simple ./dist/galaxy-24.1.2-py3-none-any.whl
  ```

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/141","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/141/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/141/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/141/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/141","id":2642051930,"node_id":"I_kwDOFe5uac6deoNa","number":141,"title":"Proposal: uncompressed input size","user":{"login":"natefoo","id":286969,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI4Njk2OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/286969?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo","html_url":"https://github.com/natefoo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-11-07T20:02:48Z","updated_at":"2024-12-10T16:49:21Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Currently `input_size` is the size of the raw input, which can be either compressed or uncompressed. When scaling memory based on input size you probably only care about the uncompressed size. But gzip does store the uncompressed size, which we could read into a separate `uncompressed_jnput_size` variable. The uncompressed size is stored in the last 4 bytes, this seems to work for me:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import os
import sys

path = sys.argv[1]

with open(path, 'rb') as f:
    f.seek(-4, os.SEEK_END)
    size = int.from_bytes(f.read(4), 'little')
    print(size)
```

The uncompressed size also isn't always set properly:

```console
nate@pdp-11% gzip -l /home/nate/work/galaxy/test-data/1.bam
         compressed        uncompressed  ratio uncompressed_name
               3592                   0   0.0% /home/nate/work/galaxy/test-data/1.bam
```

So we should have a default... actual size, or actual size * some constant factor.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/141/reactions","total_count":2,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/141/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/comments/2532268357","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/141#issuecomment-2532268357","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/141","id":2532268357,"node_id":"IC_kwDOFe5uac6W71lF","user":{"login":"natefoo","id":286969,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI4Njk2OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/286969?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo","html_url":"https://github.com/natefoo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-10T16:49:19Z","updated_at":"2024-12-10T16:49:19Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"Sure - you think this should be implemented in TPV though, not in Galaxy? When scaling memory based on input size you probably only care about the uncompressed size. But gzip does store the uncompressed size, which we could read into a separate `uncompressed_jnput_size` variable. The uncompressed size is stored in the last 4 bytes, this seems to work for me:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import os
import sys

path = sys.argv[1]

with open(path, 'rb') as f:
    f.seek(-4, os.SEEK_END)
    size = int.from_bytes(f.read(4), 'little')
    print(size)
```

The uncompressed size also isn't always set properly:

```console
nate@pdp-11% gzip -l /home/nate/work/galaxy/test-data/1.bam
         compressed        uncompressed  ratio uncompressed_name
               3592                   0   0.0% /home/nate/work/galaxy/test-data/1.bam
```

the bug**
I have a "collapse collection" step in a workflow that runs on a collection of `fastqsanger.gz` files. It follows a "Pick value" tool that seems to return a proper collection, but then the result is an `expression.json` file (see screenshot). If I run it in a simple workflow with just the collapse, I get the expected `fastqsanger.gz` file, same if the "pick value" has an existing collection as the first option. It looks like despite showing as a collection the result of the pick value picked the empty collection ? Am I not supposed to use pick value with collections? 

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: main
Commit: (run `git rev-parse HEAD` if you run this Galaxy server)

**Browser and Operating System**
Operating System: macOS
Browser: Chrome,

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Import history : https://usegalaxy.org/u/delphinel/h/test-collapse
2. Import workflow : https://usegalaxy.org/u/delphinel/w/test-collapse
3. Run the workflow with the following parameters : 
    - Hi-C Forward reads : `forward`
    - Hi-C Reverse reads : `reverse`
    - Trim Hi-C reads? : `yes` (it works fine if I select `no` here)
4. See that the final results of the collapses are `expression.json` files

**Expected behavior**
the outputs of the pick value tools should be the sorted collections if cutadapt is skipped, and running the collapse tool on them should return a fastqsanger.gz file.

**Screenshots**

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8aad9e50-b25f-424f-9a0f-93afc2cfc92b)

I'm trying to work around https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/pull/6493 which produces a collection labelled `txt,tabular`.

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**

The Galaxy Server is running version 24.1.4.dev0 , and the web client was built on Saturday Oct 26th 10:07:28 2024 GMT+2 .
Commit: ccf4353f09cc92ad6cb1e01eaf8022da9fed822e

**Browser and Operating System**
Operating System: Linux
Browser: Chrome

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:

I've tried two solutions:

- Changing the datatype of the output in the tool as it runs in a workflow. This has no effect.
- Changing the datatype after the fact via the UI (I just need it to run to completion so I can work on other tasks while I wait for the IUC bug)
  - The change datatype tab is completely missing? I only see "Attributes" and "Database/build" neither of which includes a datatype.

**Expected behavior**

I can change the datatype, either via WF, or afterwards manually to work around issues manually.

**Screenshots**

![a](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/24564617-a534-451d-9fac-0490cc5b9b1a)


**Additional context**


potentially xref #17734

A few thoughts, none of them critical:

- [Explicitly overriding `$TMP*` like this](https://github.com/goeckslab/Galaxy-Ludwig/blob/00f7da552741b6d3f576833329a9e18173ae5fd8/tools/ludwig_hyperopt.xml#L11) is probably not a good idea, Galaxy defaults to tmp space in the job directory, and admins often override this as needed for particular destinations or tools.
- I see Ludwig has this option to disable multithreading, which is exposed in the tool as an option for reproducibility purposes. But if Ludwig has an option for controlling the number of threads, I don't see it. What may happen is it gets scheduled on a node with 64 cores but is only allocated a fraction of those, assumes it can use them all, and blows up. If there is any way to pass in the number of cores, that would be great, otherwise we might have to get creative in scheduling.
- Minor, but `${dataset.element_identifier}` is typically the dataset name I believe? So this can result in some weirdness when creating those symlinks, but should be safe at least since they are quoted.
- Also minor but lot of those `pwd` calls can probably just be replaced by `.`, unless Ludwig changes the cwd internally.
- [This](https://github.com/goeckslab/Galaxy-Ludwig/blob/00f7da552741b6d3f576833329a9e18173ae5fd8/tools/ludwig_hyperopt.xml#L68) might fail under Pulsar, I am not sure if there is a "preferred" way of looking at tool stdout like this but the IUC channel probably has an answer.
- Should [this](https://github.com/goeckslab/Galaxy-Ludwig/blob/00f7da552741b6d3f576833329a9e18173ae5fd8/tools/ludwig_render_config.py#L45) be a `yaml.safe_dump()`?
- [Quoting construction in `ludwig_visualize.yml`](https://github.com/goeckslab/Galaxy-Ludwig/blob/00f7da552741b6d3f576833329a9e18173ae5fd8/tools/ludwig_visualize.xml#L20) is a bit creative but I *think* ok, but if an IUC person has a look at that as well that would be /paulocilasjr/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"## Add Ludwig-based Deep Learning Tools and Config File Generator
This PR introduces a suite of tools based on the Ludwig framework, allowing users to easily create and use deep-learning models without extensive code requirements. 

## Included Tools:
- 5 Ludwig-based tools: 
Each tool serves a unique purpose within the deep learning model lifecycle, from data preprocessing to model evaluation.
Note: the tools are currently running through Docker.

- 1 Config File Generator: 
This additional tool, though not Ludwig-based, assists users in creating the required config.yaml files. These configuration files are essential for several Ludwig tools, providing a streamlined setup process.

## Installation sequence for `tool-installers`
- [ ] Test using `@galaxybot test this`
- [ ] Inspect CI output for expected changes
- [ ] Deploy using `@galaxybot deploy this` if test install was successful
- [ ] Merge this PR

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/75","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/75/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/75/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/75/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/75","id":2634026332,"node_id":"I_kwDOHd5Rz86dAA1c","number":75,"title":"Centralized database for memory usage data","user":{"login":"natefoo","id":286969,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI4Njk2OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/286969?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo","html_url":"https://github.com/natefoo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-11-04T22:52:37Z","updated_at":"2024-11-04T22:52:38Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Right now the memory and cores in the database are a bit arbitrary - luckily, we have all the data to reason about better `mem` values (and `cores` should probably typically be `mem/4` as discussed in #60 except in cases where tools use little memory but see significant speedup with more cores).

If we all pushed our memory usage and input sizes to a centralized database we could both visualize it (similar to how I have done it one-off [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551)) and hopefully automatically make some decisions about memory values in the shared DB.

However there are some things for consideration by people who are good at statistics:

- Cutoffs for high and low memory usage (or just use 95th percentile?) since there are outliers
- Cutoffs for high and low size inputs, since there is usually a lower bound on memory that does not correlate to inputs at all
- Input compression - the mixture of compressed and uncompressed data makes input sizes as a ratio to memory usage kind of a lie
- The current memory limit can arbitrarily cut off what would be valid successful jobs and thus skew the data, and this varies by server, although we do know what the limit was for each job
- How input size affects memory usage, and of course it is rarely just input size, but also the actual data itself
- How recent of jobs to consider, since newer data is typically more useful than older data
- Tool versions to consider, since this can drastically effect memory usage, but we also don't necessarily want each `+galaxyN` version to be separate","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/75/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/75/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

of this may be controversial so I'd certainly like to hear from other usegalaxy.* admins before this is merged. =)

fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

Any tool I didn't have data for that has `cores` but no `mem` I left a comment on. fastp I don't know what to do with, since its usage is way outside of what I allocate for it, so I might try a more restricted query for of this may be controversial so I'd certainly like to hear from other usegalaxy.* admins before this is merged. =)

fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

Any tool I didn't have data for that has `cores` but no `mem` I left a comment on. fastp I don't know what to do with, since its usage is way outside of what I allocate for it, so I might try a more of this may be controversial so I'd certainly like to hear from other usegalaxy.* admins before this is merged. =)

fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

roject/tpv-shared-database/issues/76","number":76,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Add usage","user":{"login":"natefoo","id":286969,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI4Njk2OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/286969?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo","html_url":"https://github.com/natefoo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Some of this may be controversial so I'd certainly like to hear from other usegalaxy.* admins before this is merged. =)

fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

of this may be controversial so I'd certainly like to hear from other usegalaxy.* admins before this is merged. =)

fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

roject/tpv-shared-database/issues/76","number":76,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Add usage","user":{"login":"natefoo","id":286969,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI4Njk2OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/286969?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo","html_url":"https://github.com/natefoo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Some of this may be controversial so I'd certainly like to hear from other usegalaxy.* admins before this is merged. =)

fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

of this may be controversial so I'd certainly like to hear from other usegalaxy.* admins before this is merged. =)

fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

roject/tpv-shared-database/issues/76","number":76,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Add usage","user":{"login":"natefoo","id":286969,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI4Njk2OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/286969?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo","html_url":"https://github.com/natefoo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Some of this may be controversial so I'd certainly like to hear from other usegalaxy.* admins before this is merged. =)

fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

roject/tpv-shared-database/issues/76","number":76,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Add usage","user":{"login":"natefoo","id":286969,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI4Njk2OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/286969?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo","html_url":"https://github.com/natefoo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Some of this may be controversial so I'd certainly like to hear from other usegalaxy.* admins before this is merged. =)

fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

of this may be controversial so I'd certainly like to hear from other usegalaxy.* admins before this is merged. =)

fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

roject/tpv-shared-database/issues/76","number":76,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Add usage","user":{"login":"natefoo","id":286969,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI4Njk2OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/286969?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo","html_url":"https://github.com/natefoo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Some of this may be controversial so I'd certainly like to hear from other usegalaxy.* admins before this is merged. =)

fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

of this may be controversial so I'd certainly like to hear from other usegalaxy.* admins before this is merged. =)

fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

roject/tpv-shared-database/issues/76","number":76,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Add usage","user":{"login":"natefoo","id":286969,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI4Njk2OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/286969?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo","html_url":"https://github.com/natefoo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Some of this may be controversial so I'd certainly like to hear from other usegalaxy.* admins before this is merged. =)

fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

roject/tpv-shared-database/issues/76","number":76,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Add usage","user":{"login":"natefoo","id":286969,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI4Njk2OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/286969?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo","html_url":"https://github.com/natefoo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Some of this may be controversial so I'd certainly like to hear from other usegalaxy.* admins before this is merged. =)

fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

discussion in https://github.com/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/73.

~~I looked a bit at adding `#noqa` support but reading comments and figuring out the line they are associated with seemed a bit tricky. But if it's something we want I could work on that. Alternatively, `tpv lint --ignore=...` to simply ignore an entire class of warnings would be easy to implement, but then it's all-or-nothing.~~
I went ahead and added `# noqa` and command line `tpv lint --ignore=...` support. `# noqa` comments apply to the whole entity they are contained within and can either be without a specific code (in which case all warnings are ignored) or can specify codes as a comma-separated list like `# noqa: T101, T102, ...`. Inline `# noqa` comments are not supported.

IMO you'd probably not want these warnings on your local TPV config, but we probably do want them on the shared discussion in https://github.com/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/73.

~~I looked a bit at adding `#noqa` support but reading comments and figuring out the line they are associated with seemed a bit tricky. But if it's something we want I could work on that. Alternatively, `tpv lint --ignore=...` to simply ignore an entire class of warnings would be easy to implement, but then it's all-or-nothing.~~
I went ahead and added `# noqa` and command line `tpv lint --ignore=...` support. `# noqa` comments apply to the whole entity they are contained within and can either be without a specific code (in which case all warnings are ignored) or can specify codes as a comma-separated list like `# noqa: T101, T102, ...`. Inline `# noqa` comments are not supported.

discussion in https://github.com/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/73.

~~I looked a bit at adding `#noqa` support but reading comments and figuring out the line they are associated with seemed a bit tricky. But if it's something we want I could work on that. Alternatively, `tpv lint --ignore=...` to simply ignore an entire class of warnings would be easy to implement, but then it's all-or-nothing.~~
I went ahead and added `# noqa` and command line `tpv lint --ignore=...` support. `# noqa` comments apply to the whole entity they are contained within and can either be without a specific code (in which case all warnings are ignored) or can specify codes as a comma-separated list like `# noqa: T101, T102, ...`. Inline `# noqa` comments are not supported.

IMO you'd probably not want these warnings on your local TPV config, but we probably do want them on the shared {"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/75","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/75/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/75/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/75/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/75","id":2634026332,"node_id":"I_kwDOHd5Rz86dAA1c","number":75,"title":"Centralized database for memory usage data","user":{"login":"natefoo","id":286969,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI4Njk2OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/286969?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo","html_url":"https://github.com/natefoo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2024-11-04T22:52:37Z","updated_at":"2024-11-06T15:15:19Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Right now the memory and cores in the database are a bit arbitrary - luckily, we have all the data to reason about better `mem` values (and `cores` should probably typically be `mem/4` as discussed in #60 except in cases where tools use little memory but see significant speedup with more cores).

If we all pushed our memory usage and input sizes to a centralized database we could both visualize it (similar to how I have done it one-off [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551)) and hopefully automatically make some decisions about memory values in the shared DB.

However there are some things for consideration by people who are good at statistics:

- Cutoffs for high and low memory usage (or just use 95th percentile?) since there are outliers
- Cutoffs for high and low size inputs, since there is usually a lower bound on memory that does not correlate to inputs at all
- Input compression - the mixture of compressed and uncompressed data makes input sizes as a ratio to memory usage kind of a lie
- The current memory limit can arbitrarily cut off what would be valid successful jobs and thus skew the data, and this varies by server, although we do know what the limit was for each job
- How input size affects memory usage, and of course it is rarely just input size, but also the actual data itself
- How recent of jobs to consider, since newer data is typically more useful than older data
- Tool versions to consider, since this can drastically effect memory usage, but we also don't necessarily want each `+galaxyN` version to be separate","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/75/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/75/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/comments/2460033459","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/75#issuecomment-2460033459","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/75","id":2460033459,"node_id":"IC_kwDOHd5Rz86SoSGz","user":{"login":"natefoo","id":286969,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI4Njk2OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/286969?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo","html_url":"https://github.com/natefoo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-06T15:15:17Z","updated_at":"2024-11-06T15:15:17Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"Thanks for the heads up! When scaling memory based on input size you probably only care about the uncompressed size. But gzip does store the uncompressed size, which we could read into a separate `uncompressed_jnput_size` variable. The uncompressed size is stored in the last 4 bytes, this seems to work for me:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import os
import sys

path = sys.argv[1]

with open(path, 'rb') as f:
    f.seek(-4, os.SEEK_END)
    size = int.from_bytes(f.read(4), 'little')
    print(size)
```

The uncompressed size also isn't always set properly:

```console
nate@pdp-11% gzip -l /home/nate/work/galaxy/test-data/1.bam
         compressed        uncompressed  ratio uncompressed_name
               3592                   0   0.0% /home/nate/work/galaxy/test-data/1.bam
```

So we should have a default... actual size, or actual size * some constant factor.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/141/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/141/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

Adding a Chat Analysis tool powered by LLMs

Analyze and visualize a dataset with a Chat Agent.

Start a ChatAnalysis instance after selecting a dataset — we provide free access to some LLMs.

You are encouraged to use your own API keys, as the provided free access has limits. You can obtain a free API key for BambooLLM by signing up at https://pandas-ai.com/ and a free API key for Groq by signing up at https://groq.com/.

If you wish to set the OpenAI API key beforehand, go to User → Preferences → Manage Information → ChatGPT → OpenAI API Key. This way, you won’t need to set the OpenAI API key each time you use the app.

Similarly, to set the Groq API key beforehand, navigate to User → Preferences → Manage Information → Groq → API Key.

Alternatively, you can set the API key directly in the ChatAnalysis instance after it has started.
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/67c5130c-e51f-4264-933a-b5600ff77d69)

## Installation sequence for `tool-installers`
- [ ] Test using `@galaxybot test this`
- [ ] Inspect CI output for expected changes

discussion in https://github.com/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/73.

~~I looked a bit at adding `#noqa` support but reading comments and figuring out the line they are associated with seemed a bit tricky. But if it's something we want I could work on that. Alternatively, `tpv lint --ignore=...` to simply ignore an entire class of warnings would be easy to implement, but then it's all-or-nothing.~~
I went ahead and added `# noqa` and command line `tpv lint --ignore=...` support. `# noqa` comments apply to the whole entity they are contained within and can either be without a specific code (in which case all warnings are ignored) or can specify codes as a comma-separated list like `# noqa: T101, T102, ...`. Inline `# noqa` comments are not supported.

IMO you'd probably not want these warnings on your local TPV config, but we probably do want them on the shared discussion in https://github.com/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/73.

~~I looked a bit at adding `#noqa` support but reading comments and figuring out the line they are associated with seemed a bit tricky. But if it's something we want I could work on that. Alternatively, `tpv lint --ignore=...` to simply ignore an entire class of warnings would be easy to implement, but then it's all-or-nothing.~~
I went ahead and added `# noqa` and command line `tpv lint --ignore=...` support. `# noqa` comments apply to the whole entity they are contained within and can either be without a specific code (in which case all warnings are ignored) or can specify codes as a comma-separated list like `# noqa: T101, T102, ...`. Inline `# noqa` comments are not supported.

IMO you'd probably not want these warnings on your local TPV config, but we probably do want them on the shared Adding a Chat Analysis tool powered by LLMs

Analyze and visualize a dataset with a Chat Agent.

Start a ChatAnalysis instance after selecting a dataset — we provide free access to some LLMs.

You are encouraged to use your own API keys, as the provided free access has limits. You can obtain a free API key for BambooLLM by signing up at https://pandas-ai.com/ and a free API key for Groq by signing up at https://groq.com/.

If you wish to set the OpenAI API key beforehand, go to User → Preferences → Manage Information → ChatGPT → OpenAI API Key. This way, you won’t need to set the OpenAI API key each time you use the app.

Similarly, to set the Groq API key beforehand, navigate to User → Preferences → Manage Information → Groq → API Key.

Alternatively, you can set the API key directly in the ChatAnalysis instance after it has started.
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/67c5130c-e51f-4264-933a-b5600ff77d69)

## Installation sequence for `tool-installers`
- [ ] Test using `@galaxybot test this`
- [ ] Inspect CI output for expected changes
- [ ] Deploy using `@galaxybot deploy this` if test install was successful
- [ ] Merge this PR

Adding a Chat Analysis tool powered by LLMs

Analyze and visualize a dataset with a Chat Agent.

Start a ChatAnalysis instance after selecting a dataset — we provide free access to some LLMs.

You are encouraged to use your own API keys, as the provided free access has limits. You can obtain a free API key for BambooLLM by signing up at https://pandas-ai.com/ and a free API key for Groq by signing up at https://groq.com/.

If you wish to set the OpenAI API key beforehand, go to User → Preferences → Manage Information → ChatGPT → OpenAI API Key. This way, you won’t need to set the OpenAI API key each time you use the app.

Similarly, to set the Groq API key beforehand, navigate to User → Preferences → Manage Information → Groq → API Key.

Alternatively, you can set the API key directly in the ChatAnalysis instance after it has started.
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/67c5130c-e51f-4264-933a-b5600ff77d69)

## Installation sequence for `tool-installers`
- [ ] Test using `@galaxybot test this`
- [ ] Inspect CI output for expected changes
- [ ] Deploy using `@galaxybot deploy this` if test install was successful
- [ ] Merge this PR

Adding a Chat Analysis tool powered by LLMs

Analyze and visualize a dataset with a Chat Agent.

Start a ChatAnalysis instance after selecting a dataset — we provide free access to some LLMs.

You are encouraged to use your own API keys, as the provided free access has limits. You can obtain a free API key for BambooLLM by signing up at https://pandas-ai.com/ and a free API key for Groq by signing up at https://groq.com/.

If you wish to set the OpenAI API key beforehand, go to User → Preferences → Manage Information → ChatGPT → OpenAI API Key. This way, you won’t need to set the OpenAI API key each time you use the app.

Similarly, to set the Groq API key beforehand, navigate to User → Preferences → Manage Information → Groq → API Key.

Alternatively, you can set the API key directly in the ChatAnalysis instance after it has started.
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/67c5130c-e51f-4264-933a-b5600ff77d69)

## Installation sequence for `tool-installers`
- [ ] Test using `@galaxybot test this`
- [ ] Inspect CI output for expected changes
- [ ] Deploy using `@galaxybot deploy this` if test install was successful
- [ ] Merge this PR

of this may be controversial so I'd certainly like to hear from other usegalaxy.* admins before this is merged. =)

fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

Any tool I didn't have data for that has `cores` but no `mem` I left a comment on. fastp I don't know what to do with, since its usage is way outside of what I allocate for it, so I might try a more restricted query for fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

Any tool I didn't have data for that has `cores` but no `mem` I left a comment on. fastp I don't know what to do with, since its usage is way outside of what I allocate for it, so I might try a more pins versions of all the `galaxy*` packages, but to mimic the current production best practice/`run.sh`, should we add all of `pinned-requirements.txt`?~~ yes

I tried adding all of `pinned-requirements.txt` to `install_requires` but it fails because `galaxy-data` 24.1.2 (and all earlier versions of it) have a pin on `social-auth-core==4.0.3` while the `pinned-requirements.txt` version for 24.1.2 is 4.5.4. We should unpin this in `galaxy-data` but it won't be possible to build a fully pinned `galaxy` metapackage until we unpin that and publish 24.1.3 packages.

## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  ```sh-session
  cd package/galaxy
  python -m build
  pip install --extra-index-url https://wheels.galaxyproject.org/simple ./dist/galaxy-24.1.2-py3-none-any.whl
  ```

{"action":"closed","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15924","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15924/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15924/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15924/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15924","id":1662842875,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc5jHPf7","number":15924,"title":"Enabling the cgroup metrics plugin on systems with cgroupv2 causes jobs to fail","user":{"login":"natefoo","id":286969,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI4Njk2OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/286969?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo","html_url":"https://github.com/natefoo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2023-04-11T16:18:44Z","updated_at":"2024-10-01T16:06:30Z","closed_at":"2024-10-01T16:06:29Z","author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
Newer Linuxes (Ubuntu 22.04+, EL9+) now mount cgroupv2 instead of cgroupv1. Cgroupv2 has a completely different hierarchy, so the plugin's path assumptions are wrong. Because the last statement in the job script is from the plugin and it returns non-zero, this fails the entire job for DRMs like Slurm that use the exit code of the job script to determine the job exit status.

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: all

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Install Galaxy on Ubuntu 22.04
2. Enable the cgroup metrics plugin
3. Configure Galaxy to run jobs on Slurm
4. Run a job
5. Error

**Expected behavior**
Jobs do not fail when the cgroup metrics plugin is enabled and the job runs on a host with cgroupv2 mounted.

**Screenshots**
N/A

**Additional context**
cgroupv2 is fairly useless for the one thing that is of interest to Galaxy admins: it has no measurement equivalent to cgroupv1's `memory.(memsw.)max_usage_in_bytes` (max memory usage recorded). Thus it is recommended that cgroupv2 be disabled and cgroupv1 be mounted instead. This can be done by setting the kernel options:
```
systemd.unified_cgroup_hierarchy=0 systemd.legacy_systemd_cgroup_controller=1
```
Typically this is achieved by appending the options to `GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT` in `/etc/default/grub`, running `update-grub` as root, and rebooting. Of course, this won't be an option for people who do not admin their own clusters.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15924/reactions","total_count":2,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":2,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15924/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"}}

error log collection fails","user":{"login":"natefoo","id":286969,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI4Njk2OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/286969?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo","html_url":"https://github.com/natefoo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-10-01T20:29:49Z","updated_at":"2024-10-01T20:29:49Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"The wrapper attempts to [collect the parallel log outputs upon failure](https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/blob/f28d66c0bcad8ac29be2afec28bc639317239775/tools/lofreq/lofreq_call.xml#L94), but the quotes around the glob expression prevent it from actually matching and expanding.

It should be safe to just quote the var instead

## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. ```console
      $ mulled-hash --hash conda bedtools=2.30.0,samtools=1.9
      mulled-v1-ca195b12c14e35565e393a2d07f2deac7610d8126cc3460d217504efd11d4347
      ```

With 56 cores pigz decreased the compression runtime of a 24 GB meryldb from an estimated 30 hours to 9 minutes.

I also added `m` to the untar options so that mod times are not preserved. This can be useful when working with old datasets on HPC systems that have aggressive scratch cleanup.

Tested on the command line but not in the tools so I am relying on tool tests to catch my mistakes. =)

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18972","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18972/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18972/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18972/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18972","id":2578297080,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc6ZrbD4","number":18972,"title":"depot.galaxyproject.org server is very slow","user":{"login":"mashehu","id":6169021,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxNjkwMjE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/6169021?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mashehu","html_url":"https://github.com/mashehu","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mashehu/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mashehu/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mashehu/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mashehu/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mashehu/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mashehu/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mashehu/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mashehu/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mashehu/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-10-10T09:59:29Z","updated_at":"2024-10-11T18:13:31Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
Downloading singularity images from depot.galaxyproject.org is very slow ~20-50KiB/s.

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: (check <galaxy_url>/api/version if you don't know)
Commit: (run `git rev-parse HEAD` if you run this Galaxy server)

**Browser and Operating System**
Operating System: macOS
Browser: Firefox, Chrome, Safari

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. run `singularity pull  --name depot.galaxyproject.org-singularity-multiqc-1.25.1--pyhdfd78af_0.img.pulling.1728550459893 https://depot.galaxyproject.org/singularity/multiqc:1.25.1--pyhdfd78af_0`

**Expected behavior**
Faster download speed

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

Because TPV fail rules intentionally fail jobs with `JobMappingException` and we probably *don't* want those in Sentry, Marius suggested:

> I would  maybe add a `log_level` attribute to `JobMappingException` and have those fail rules be raised with `raise JobMappingException("bla", log_level="info")`. Or split the `JobMappingException` into `JobMappingUnsatisfiable` and `JobMappingException` or something like that?

Which would be good for dev (and TPV would need to change its raise).

{"action":"closed","number":18992,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/18992","id":2123472625,"node_id":"PR_kwDOAdw-Nc5-kZ7x","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/18992","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/18992.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/18992.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18992","number":18992,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"[24.1] Log `JobMappingException`s at error level rather than debug","user":{"login":"natefoo","id":286969,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI4Njk2OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/286969?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo","html_url":"https://github.com/natefoo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This allows us to capture them in Sentry. Because TPV fail rules intentionally fail jobs with `JobMappingException` and we probably *don't* want those in Sentry, Marius suggested:

> I would  maybe add a `log_level` attribute to `JobMappingException` and have those fail rules be raised with `raise JobMappingException("bla", log_level="info")`. Or split the `JobMappingException` into `JobMappingUnsatisfiable` and `JobMappingException` or something like that?

Which would be good for dev (and TPV would need to change its raise).

the script I used to check usage to allocation efficiency, I'll try to get this converted to gxadmin so we can dump to influx for monitoring:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import argparse
import statistics

import psycopg2


INTERVAL = '1 week'
PG_DBNAME = 'galaxy_main'
MEM_FLOOR = 16
RUNTIME_FLOOR = 300
SQL = """
SELECT
    t.id,
    t.tool_id,
    t.mem_allocated,
    t.mem_used
FROM (
    SELECT
        j.id,
        j.tool_id,
        (SELECT metric_value FROM job_metric_numeric WHERE job_id = j.id AND metric_name = 'galaxy_memory_mb') * pow(1024, 2) AS mem_allocated,
        (SELECT metric_value FROM job_metric_numeric WHERE job_id = j.id AND metric_name = 'memory.peak') AS mem_used,
        (SELECT metric_value FROM job_metric_numeric WHERE job_id = j.id AND metric_name = 'runtime_seconds') AS runtime
    FROM
        job j
    WHERE
        j.update_time > timezone('UTC', now()) - %(interval)s::INTERVAL
        AND j.state = 'ok'
) AS t
WHERE
    t.mem_allocated >= (%(mem_floor)s * pow(1024, 3))
    AND t.runtime > %(runtime_floor)s
"""


def get_runtime_data(args):
    args = {
        "interval": args.interval,
        "mem_floor": args.mem_floor,
        "runtime_floor": args.runtime_floor,
    }
    conn = psycopg2.connect(dbname=PG_DBNAME)
    cur = conn.cursor()
    cur.execute(SQL, args)
    return cur.fetchall()


def calculate_ratios(rows):
    total = len(rows)
    missing = 0
    tools = {}
    for row in rows:
        job_id, tool_id, mem_allocated, mem_used = row
        if not mem_allocated or not mem_used:
            missing += 1
            continue
        if tool_id not in tools:
            tools[tool_id] = []
        tools[tool_id].append(float(mem_used) / mem_allocated)

    means = {}
    for tool_id, ratios in tools.items():
        means[tool_id] = statistics.mean(ratios)

    for tool_id in sorted(means, key=means.get, reverse=True):
        print(f"{tool_id}: {means[tool_id]*100:0.2f}%")

    print("")
    print(f"{(missing/total)*100:0.2f}% missing")


parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
parser.add_argument("--interval", "-i", default=INTERVAL, help="Age of jobs to check")
parser.add_argument("--mem-floor", "-m", default=MEM_FLOOR, help="Memory floor (GB), ignore jobs that used less")
parser.add_argument("--runtime-floor", "-r", default=RUNTIME_FLOOR, help="Runtime floor (seconds), ignore jobs that ran for less")
args = parser.parse_args()
rows = get_runtime_data(args)
calculate_ratios(rows)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/comments/2415329163/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

## Sept 19, 2024

To make Galaxy useful for BRC community we need to integrate reference data for all 785 species by downloading sequence data and building indices for:
- Bowtie 1 and 2
- BWA-mem, and bwa-mem2
- STAR
- hisat2
- SNPeff databases
- ...

Sept 26, 2024

@natefoo started the process:

- Galaxy dbKeys will be set to GenBank or RefSeq IDs (`assembly version ID` column in https://brc-analytics.org/data/organisms)
- Names (in tool dropdowns) will be set as UCSC Names

## Sept 19, 2024

To make Galaxy useful for BRC community we need to integrate reference data for all 785 species by downloading sequence data and building indices for:
- Bowtie 1 and 2
- BWA-mem, and bwa-mem2
- STAR
- hisat2
- SNPeff databases
- ...
To do this we need a list of all UCSC dbKeys for these
